content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

September 10, 1981


Sherry Blair
2896 Alide Street
Hayward, CA 94551

Dear Ms. Blair:

This in in reply to your letter dated August 21, 1981, regarding
the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act in connection with your candidacy for the Hayward Unified School District
Board of Governors.

Your interpretation of the filing requirements is correct.
However, I would like to clarify that although personal funds used for
the filing fee or statement of qualifications are not included in
calculating whether $500 in expenditures have been made for the purpose
of determining whether a Form 470 may be filed (Government Code Section
84205(a)), this payment does come within the definition of
"expenditure." Government Code Section 84200 requires the filing of a
semi-annual campaign statement if any activity has occured during the
half-year covered.

Enclosed is a Form 470 - Candidate's and Officeholder's Campaign
Statement-Short Form, which you may file as a semi-annual campaign
statement so long as you do not raise or spend $500 or more during the
half-year.

Please call me collect at the above number for the Technical Assistance Division if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

[illegible]

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

Sherry Blair
22896 Alice Street
Hayward, Ca. 94541

Fair Political Practices Commission          August 21, 1981
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

I have filed a declaration of candidacy for election to
the office of member of the governing board of the Hayward
Unified School District. The election will be held Nov. 3, 1981.

It is my plan not to receive or spend $500.00 or more
during the entire process. I have filed a statement of qualifications, the cost of which is unknown.

In reading the "1981 Informational manual on campaign
disclosuresprovisions of the political reform act" it appears
that under these conditions, the following applies:

1. I do not need a committee or a treasurer.

2. I do not need to file threshold statements.

3. I must file semi-annual statements only if I receive or
make expenditures. Personal funds spent on the statement
of qualifications are not counted as expenditures.

I would appreciate your comments particularly if my
interpretation is incorrect.

Thank you very much.

Sherry Blair

sections.question: I have filed a declaration of candidacy for election to the office of member of the governing board of the Hayward Unified School District. The election will be held Nov. 3, 1981. It is my plan not to receive or spend $500.00 or more during the entire process. I have filed a statement of qualifications, the cost of which is unknown. In reading the "1981 Informational manual on campaign disclosuresprovisions of the political reform act" it appears that under these conditions, the following applies: 1. I do not need a committee or a treasurer. 2. I do not need to file threshold statements. 3. I must file semi-annual statements only if I receive or make expenditures. Personal funds spent on the statement of qualifications are not counted as expenditures. I would appreciate your comments particularily if my interpretation is incorrect.
sections.conclusion: Your interpretation of the filing requirements is correct. However, I would like to clarify that although personal funds used for the filing fee or statement of qualifications are not included in calculating whether $500 in expenditures have been made for the purpose of determining whether a Form 470 may be filed (Government Code Section 84205 (a)), this payment does come within the definition of "expenditure." Government Code Section 84200 requires the filing of a semi-annual campaign statement if any activity has occurred during the half-year covered. Enclosed is a Form 470 - Candidate's and Officeholder's Campaign Statement-Short Form, which you may file as a semi-annual campaign statement so long as you do not raise or spend $500 or more during the half-year.
sections.question_synthetic: A candidate for the Hayward Unified School District Board seeks clarification on campaign disclosure filing requirements under the Political Reform Act, specifically whether personal funds spent on statement of qualifications count as expenditures and whether she must file forms if she spends less than $500.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The candidate's interpretation is largely correct, but the FPPC clarifies that while personal funds for statement of qualifications don't count toward the $500 threshold for Form 470 filing, they do constitute an "expenditure" under the definition. Semi-annual statements must be filed if any activity occurs during the half-year, and the short Form 470 can be used if the candidate does not raise or spend $500 or more.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated August 21, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (dated September 10, 1981). Response from Jeanne Pritchard, Political Reform Consultant. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION headers present; question and conclusion extracted from letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I have filed a declaration of candidacy for election to the office of member of the governing board of the Hayward Unified School District. The election will be held Nov. 3, 1981. It is my plan not to receive or spend $500.00 or more during the entire process. I have filed a statement of qualifications, the cost of which is unknown. In reading the "1981 Informational manual on campaign disclosuresprovisions of the political reform act" it appears that under these conditions, the following applies: 1. I do not need a committee or a treasurer. 2. I do not need to file threshold statements. 3. I must file semi-annual statements only if I receive or make expenditures. Personal funds spent on the statement of qualifications are not counted as expenditures. I would appreciate your comments particularily if my interpretation is incorrect.

CONCLUSION: Your interpretation of the filing requirements is correct. However, I would like to clarify that although personal funds used for the filing fee or statement of qualifications are not included in calculating whether $500 in expenditures have been made for the purpose of determining whether a Form 470 may be filed (Government Code Section 84205 (a)), this payment does come within the definition of "expenditure." Government Code Section 84200 requires the filing of a semi-annual campaign statement if any activity has occurred during the half-year covered. Enclosed is a Form 470 - Candidate's and Officeholder's Campaign Statement-Short Form, which you may file as a semi-annual campaign statement so long as you do not raise or spend $500 or more during the half-year.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a school board candidate's inquiry about campaign disclosure requirements, confirming her interpretation is correct but clarifying that personal funds for qualifications count as expenditures and semi-annual statements must be filed if any activity occurs during the half-year.
